questions.q: In which town is the Mahabodhi Temple located?
questions.explanation: The Mahabodhi Temple stands at Bodh Gaya, in the Indian state of Bihar.

questions.q: The Mahabodhi Temple is sacred to which religion?
questions.explanation: It marks the spot where the Buddha attained enlightenment, making it one of Buddhism's holiest sites.

questions.q: What famous tree is associated with the Mahabodhi Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple stands beside the Bodhi tree, under which the Buddha is said to have attained enlightenment.

questions.q: In which Indian state is the Mahabodhi Temple located?
questions.explanation: Bodh Gaya and the Mahabodhi Temple are located in Bihar.

questions.q: What major event in the Buddha's life is the Mahabodhi Temple believed to commemorate?
questions.explanation: The site commemorates the Buddha's attainment of enlightenment (Bodhi) under the Bodhi tree.

questions.q: What kind of monument is the Mahabodhi Temple?
questions.explanation: It is a Buddhist temple complex centered on the place of the Buddha's enlightenment.

questions.q: The Mahabodhi Temple is part of which prestigious international heritage designation?
questions.explanation: The Mahabodhi Temple Complex was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2002.

questions.q: Which ancient Indian emperor is traditionally credited with building the first shrine at Bodh Gaya?
questions.explanation: The Mauryan emperor Ashoka is traditionally credited with establishing the first shrine at the site in the 3rd century BCE.

questions.q: What is the dominant feature of the Mahabodhi Temple's silhouette?
questions.explanation: The temple is dominated by a tall, pyramidal spire (shikhara) rising above the main shrine.

questions.q: Who is the central religious figure linked to the Mahabodhi Temple?
questions.explanation: The temple is dedicated to and commemorates Gautama Buddha's enlightenment.

questions.q: In which year was the Mahabodhi Temple Complex inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site?
questions.explanation: The Mahabodhi Temple Complex was inscribed on the UNESCO World Heritage List in 2002.

questions.q: The species of the Bodhi tree at Bodh Gaya is which kind of fig?
questions.explanation: The Bodhi tree is a sacred fig, Ficus religiosa, also known as the pipal tree.

questions.q: The main tower of the present Mahabodhi Temple rises to roughly what height?
questions.explanation: The central tower of the Mahabodhi Temple rises to approximately 50 metres in height.

questions.q: The present grand structure of the Mahabodhi Temple is generally dated to which period?
options: The Gupta period (around the 5th-6th century CE)
questions.explanation: The surviving brick temple in its essential form is dated to the Gupta period, around the 5th-6th century CE.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of the present Mahabodhi Temple?
questions.explanation: The Mahabodhi Temple is one of the earliest and largest Buddhist temples built entirely of brick still standing in India.

questions.q: The early polished sandstone railing fragments preserved at the site are stylistically linked to which period?
questions.explanation: Some of the early stone railings around the temple are attributed to the Shunga period, around the 1st century BCE.

questions.q: Which Chinese pilgrim left a detailed account of Bodh Gaya after visiting in the 7th century CE?
questions.explanation: Xuanzang (Hsuan-tsang) visited Bodh Gaya in the 7th century and described the temple in his travel records.

questions.q: The slab marking the exact spot of the Buddha's enlightenment beside the tree is known as the:
questions.explanation: The Vajrasana, or Diamond Throne, is the stone seat marking where the Buddha sat in meditation when he attained enlightenment.

questions.q: The four smaller corner towers of the Mahabodhi Temple echo what feature?
questions.explanation: Four smaller towers stand at the corners, repeating the design of the tall central spire.

questions.q: Which British archaeologist led major documentation and restoration efforts at the Mahabodhi Temple in the 19th century?
questions.explanation: Alexander Cunningham, founder of the Archaeological Survey of India, undertook extensive survey and restoration work at the temple.

questions.q: The present Bodhi tree at Bodh Gaya is believed to descend from the original through a famous offshoot grown in:
questions.explanation: The sacred fig at Anuradhapura, Sri Lanka, was grown from a cutting of the original Bodhi tree and itself served as a source for replanting at Bodh Gaya.

questions.q: Which reformer founded the Maha Bodhi Society in 1891 to restore Buddhist links to the temple?
questions.explanation: The Sri Lankan reformer Anagarika Dharmapala founded the Maha Bodhi Society in 1891 to campaign for the restoration of Bodh Gaya as a Buddhist site.

questions.q: The seven sacred spots marking the weeks the Buddha spent after enlightenment are located:
options: Within or near the Mahabodhi Temple complex
questions.explanation: The seven spots commemorating the seven weeks the Buddha spent in the vicinity after his enlightenment are found within and around the Mahabodhi Temple complex.

questions.q: The 'Animisalochana' spot in the complex commemorates which post-enlightenment act of the Buddha?
options: Gazing at the Bodhi tree without blinking for a week
questions.explanation: The Animisalochana Chaitya marks where the Buddha is said to have stood gazing at the Bodhi tree in gratitude for a full week without blinking.

questions.q: The 'Ratnachakrama' or Jewel Walk in the complex is a raised platform marking where the Buddha:
questions.explanation: The Ratnachakrama is a raised stone platform with carved lotuses marking the path where the Buddha is said to have walked in meditation.

questions.q: Buddhist missions from which distant kingdom are recorded as having sponsored repairs at Bodh Gaya in the medieval and later periods?
questions.explanation: Burmese (Myanmar) Buddhist missions are recorded as having sponsored repairs and restorations at the Mahabodhi Temple over the centuries.

questions.q: Which dynasty, ruling eastern India from the 8th to 12th centuries, was a major patron of Buddhism that supported Bodh Gaya?
questions.explanation: The Pala dynasty of Bengal and Bihar were notable patrons of Buddhism and supported sites including Bodh Gaya.

questions.q: The legislation that established a management committee for the temple, the Bodh Gaya Temple Act, was passed in:
questions.explanation: The Bodh Gaya Temple Act of 1949 created a management committee to oversee the temple's administration.

questions.q: Architecturally, the tall straight-edged pyramidal spire of the Mahabodhi Temple is significant as an early form of which tower type?
options: A straight-sided shikhara antecedent to later North Indian temple towers
questions.explanation: The temple's tall, straight-sided tapering tower is considered an important early form influencing later North Indian temple superstructures.

questions.q: A famous replica of the Mahabodhi Temple's distinctive form was built in which distant Buddhist city?
questions.explanation: The Mahabodhi Temple of Bagan in Myanmar was modeled on the Bodh Gaya original, reflecting its wide influence.
